'''Arpor-Lan''' was the name of a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoids]] with horned chins. Coming from [[Arporatal-Lanin]], Arpor-Lans were famous through [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] and trackers. During the last centuries of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Arpor-Lan were [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] to the [[Noble/Legends|noble]] houses ruling the [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex]]. Later, some Arpor-Lan became [[Officer/Legends|officers]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], such as [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenants]] [[Deeve]] and [[Fraj T'lin]]

==Biology and Appearance==
Arpor-Lan were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoids]] sharing a general physical outline with [[Human/Legends|Humans]]: They had two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and two legs, and continued effort made them hurt. They were known to sweat near their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and they had a versatile enough [[Tongue/Legends|tongue]] to manipulate the [[monitor pad]]s of a [[Breath mask/Legends|breath mask]]. They were similar enough to Humans to use Human-oriented technology such as a [[Breath mask/Legends|face breather]].<ref name="G">{{WEGCite|story=A Typical Survey|book=Galaxy Guide 8: Scouts}}</ref> An Arpor-Lan had hair on the top of the head, like a Human.<ref name="I" />

However, the face showed two important disimilarities. The eyebrows of an Arpor-Lan showed external bones as plaques,<ref name="I" /> and the chin showed stubby horns.<ref name="I" /><ref name="R">''[[Rebel Force: Renegade]]''</ref> Some Arpor-Lan tugged their horns or cleared their throats when nervous.<ref name="R" /> Arpor-Lan were not immune to phobias.<ref name="G" />

==Society and Culture==
Arpor-Lan's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was [[Arporatal-Lanin]], in the [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The native Arpor-Lan were [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy-wide]] famous as hunters, [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] and trackers.<ref name="A" />

In times of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Arpor-Lan traveled through the galaxy.<ref name="I" /><ref name="G" /> A number of them were armed and instinctively drew a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] when they felt threatened.<ref name="G" />

==History==
Arpor-Lan were the native [[Species/Legends|species]] of Arporatal-Lanin, a planet that had not been discovered by the galactic community. Circa [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], the tycoon [[Thull Vandron]] gathered seventeen [[Noble/Legends|noble]] houses of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and took them to a hidden cluster of worlds. These planets were divided in two groups, [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex]] and [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex]]. Arportal-Lanin was in Juvex, an area that was assigned to [[House Carbinol|Houses Carbinol]], [[House Halliikeenovich|Hallikeenovich]], [[House Kassido|Kassido]], [[House Kellermin|Kellermin]], [[House Mekuun|Mekuun]], [[House Pilor|Pilor]], [[House Sisemon|Sisemon]] and [[House Sreethyn|Sreethyn]]. These aristocrats found native species in their worlds, and spent one century bending them using [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]]. Arporatal-Lanin and the other planets in Juvex became property of the aristocrats.<ref name="A" />

By [[700 BBY]], all the native species of Juvex, including the Arpor-Lan, had been [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] for so long that they deemed freedom an old myth. But by then the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] found Senex and Juvex and began a restless relationship with the ruling houses. Republic [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] heard rumors about the enslaved locals. In [[553 BBY]], a Republic [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] began to free some of the species in the area. During the [[Fourth Nantama Synod]], the Houses joined the Republic, with Senex and Juvex becoming [[Sector/Legends|sectors]].<ref name="A" />

However, the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] was unhappy with the slaving ways of the Houses, as they were violating the [[Rights of Sentience]]. The Houses demanded that they could keep that integral part of their culture. This led to a number of disputes during the following centuries.<ref name="A" />

===Arpor-Lan in the galaxy===
In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], a number of Arpor-Lan had joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Fraj T'lin]], an Arpor-Lan, served in the Rebel base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] soon after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="R" /> In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], the premier scout Lieutenant [[Deeve]] was one of the more famous Arpor-Lan in the Alliance, and he also worked for [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]].<ref name="I" /> During the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Deeve worked for the [[Bureau of Scouting and Exploration Services]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="G" />

[[File:NRSSDeeve.png|thumb|right|Deeve, agent of NRSS]]

==Behind the scenes==
While Deeve first appeared in ''[[Galaxy Guide 8: Scouts]]'' ([[1993]]), his species was only identified in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' ([[1995]]). The graphic depiction of Deeve in this last book shares several traits with depictions of the [[Zygerrian/Legends|Zygerrians]].
